of the implementation is quite important for us.
Um, and we will,
and so part of the challenge is starting with ourselves
and creating the confidence to be able to do that
amongst ourselves and our own and being very diligent
and deliberate, um,
and disciplined actually in how we present, um,
both our second opinion advice,
but also our primary advice to government
and making sure, um,
that we are being exemplars of this work.
And then alongside that is mentioned working with agencies.
We, we, we we're going with the coalition
of the willing at the moment to see this as an opportunity.
Well, those are important engagements
because it could be well be that over the next 12 months,
there might be some refinements that are made as a result
of the engagements to the guidance.
So that's important. Um,
but we'll also look to,
they should see an improvement in
potentially feedback from ministers.
What, uh, might be that, um,
they can see an improvement in the advice that's coming, um,
through, because of the work that they're applying.
So that's, you know, that that's part of the plan.
But the other part is also we do, we do a significant amount
of second opinion advice

or what we are moving to is, for instance,
if we get advice from the min, uh, requests for, um,
support from the Ministry for Environment, for example,
over time, we would want to be able to say back to them,
these are the themes that we are seeing in terms
of the advice we are providing to you, and
therefore, um, if there's an opportunity
to be able to work with you.
So we are not having to give back to you the things
that ordinarily should be part
of your policy process anyway.
So it's how you kind of create a way of being able
to enable people to do the thinking themselves,
inquire into the thinking themselves without us necessarily
having to, you know, to do that all the time.
Um, so those are the, there will be
challenges, no doubt about it.
Um, but what we are trying to do is be quite reasonable,
reasonable and considered in, uh, moving forward
and applying this, in this, applying this work and,
and as I say, starting with ourselves
and with those who, who want to work with us on this mahi.
So you, you plan to have a, um, a dashboard of, of,
or what's the method for you measuring success
with this tool moving forward?
Yeah, um, we, we, we are planning to, uh, we've,
we've designed a set of criteria, um, that we are currently,
we've, we've kept in draft
because we want to test the criteria, what success,
what this would look like if we saw it in practice.
And our intention is, is that by using that criteria,
we would then be able to apply, um, uh, we, we would,
we would be able to pro provide a status report in, in terms
of, uh, a dashboard, um, for reporting.
There's two parts to it.
It is the, um, it is both the, uh, the application
of the ability to be able
to feel comfortable in applying the, the guidance.
Um, but secondly, it is then also, well, the guidance
actually leading to approved outcomes for, um, for people,
for the, uh, yeah,
for the reason in which the policy has been designed.
So that's probably a little bit longer, that is longer term.
Uh, 'cause you wanna be able to, uh, uh, apply the,
it's about applying the, the work in the context of the mahi
that the, that the agencies are engaged with.
So it's a a two-pronged approach in terms of the,
our monitoring, um, the monitoring that we would,
that we're intending to do.
The, um, Dave, you, you sort of, uh, articulated, uh,
the problem, this is endeavoring to solve itself,
um, in, in primarily around around policy
and delivery as Bernie talked to also.
Um, it's, I suppose my question is around like
how do you measure if it's a successful policy
or if it's not successful
and be, I I'm just mindful of the role
that this committee plays in, in, uh, improving performance,
holding mutual account, all the people, the above.
Mm-hmm. Uh, I'm trying to identify how you can assist us
with that so that we can do our money, uh, with regards
to our scrutinizing, et cetera itself.
Do you understand the question I'm trying to ask you?
I do. Um, the, the point,
I think it's, it's the opportunity.
The first point is some clarity for the committee.
Um, so when you are engaging with the community,
being clear about who you are engaging with
and for what purpose,
because at the moment, um, often that's not clear.
I don't know if it's always been clear in the select
committee, but certainly as public servants, that's not,
hasn't always been clear in some of the examples.
So it's providing that clarity.
And I, I think out of this also understanding
that not everything is just about,
um, Maori as constituents
or members of Hapu and iwi.
'cause sometimes we neglect to realize that this
responsibilities here as public servants
and for the government to serve citizens that are Maori.
Now, that has happened for many years.
That happened after World War ii,
where Maori soldiers came back
and didn't receive the same sort of attention
as other New Zealand citizens who bought in the war.
Yeah. So it's, it's just getting that,
um, that balance.
So we don't push everything to one side, one dimensional,
and we lift and we understand there's two components here.
And certainly from a a citizen perspective, if I look across
the public service
and the statistics, we are not serving Maori citizens Well,
that's just a fact.
So, so understanding, well, it's not all pushed into there.
We, we've really gotta highlight the Maori citizen component
as an example, and some clarity on when we are doing
that, why we are doing that.
Because you'd want to have some balance.
Mm-hmm. There, there would be a mix
of both quantitative

So developing really nuanced ways
and being able to express how that was, um, uh,
adopted using te.
Okay. Alright.
Are there any last questions
before I close this part of the session?
If not, I'll just close by saying I, I, I think this is, uh,
I think this is pretty exciting.

and it's articulated in the documentation here,
this is kind long gestation to get to where we have, uh,

us here today with regards to that.
Um, and, uh, I will bring the briefing to a, uh, to a close.
We'll be seeing you, a number
of you again shortly type thing.
But thank you very much
for this session that we've had today.

16 July 2025 | Māori Affairs Committee
Briefing on Te Tautuhi-o-Rongo (Māori Macro Policy Framework)
